merger_name: Black Rhino Group – Granville Tavern and Motel and Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars in Granville, QLD

acquirers.name: The Trustee for It Is What It Is (Granville) Trust

merger_description:
The Black Rhino Group is a national hospitality group operating 48 venues across Queensland, Victoria and South Australia. The Black Rhino Group operates hospitality venues, including hotels, bottleshops, accommodation, and food and beverage offerings, with a particular focus on gaming, wagering, family bistros, sports bars, retail liquor, entertainment and accommodation. Black Rhino (Granville) Pty Ltd and the trustee for the It Is What It Is (Granville) Trust are part of the Black Rhino Group. The Black Rhino Group has 5 licensed venues and locations in Queensland.

Granville Hotel Qld Pty Ltd (**Vendor**) owns and operates the freehold land, buildings, licences and business assets (both tangible and intangible) of:

* Granville Tavern and Motel; and
* Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars.

Granville Tavern and Motel is a commercial hotel motel business which offers dining, accommodation and gaming machines. Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars is a bottle shop that offers packaged liquor for off-premises consumption. Both businesses are based in Granville, Queensland.

Black Rhino (Granville) Pty Ltd and the trustee for the It Is What It Is (Granville) Trust propose to acquire 100% of the Vendor’s interest in the aforementioned businesses, together with the relevant freehold from which the businesses operate.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Black Rhino (Granville) Pty Ltd and the trustee for the It Is What It Is
(Granville) Trust, as part of the Black Rhino Group, applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of
Granville Hotel Qld Pty Ltd’s interest in the Granville Tavern and
Motel and the Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars businesses,
together with the relevant freehold land from which the businesses
operate, as described in the transaction documents provided as part
of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The Black Rhino Group is a national hospitality group operating 48
venues across Queensland, Victoria and South Australia. The Black
Rhino Group operate licensed venues, including hotels, bottleshops,
accommodation, and food and beverage offerings, with a particular
focus on gaming, wagering, family bistros, sports bars, retail liquor,
entertainment and accommodation. Black Rhino (Granville) Pty Ltd
and the trustee for the It Is What It Is (Granville) Trust are part of the
Black Rhino Group. The Black Rhino Group has 5 licensed venues
and locations in Queensland.
The Granville Tavern and Motel is a commercial hotel motel
business which offers dining, accommodation and gaming
machines. The Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars is a bottle
shop that offers packaged liquor for off-premises consumption. Both
businesses are based in Granville, a suburb of Maryborough in
Queensland’s Fraser Coast Region.
The Black Rhino Group’s closest venue to the Granville Tavern and
Motel and Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars is approximately
390km away by drive.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the ACCC considers that the
Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular, there is no local overlap between the Black
Rhino Group and the Granville Tavern and Model and the Cambridge
Street Bottle Shop & Cellars in the supply of on-premises hospitality
products and services (including liquor for on-site consumption,
dining and gaming), accommodation, or packaged liquor for offsite
consumption, in Granville and surrounding areas.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
